ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag. The depicted figure is an anonymous cultist — a follower of Valgavoth, not Valgavoth himself. Valgavoth character node already in corpus confirms: no corpus card currently depicts Valgavoth in art; the two named printings (DSK-120 Terror Eater, DSC-6 Harrower of Souls) are not in inventory. This card depicts a follower only.

trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK) released September 27, 2024. The set is set on the plane of Duskmourn, a world consumed by a sentient House grown from the body of the moth-demon Valgavoth as he fed on the survivors' fear. Valgavoth's Faithful depicts one of the human cultists who serve him willingly inside the House — counterpart to the 'Glimmer' faction of survivors who resist his influence. [Scryfall: released_at 2024-09-27; Valgavoth character node in corpus]

Flavor text (verbatim): "That's the one good thing about these cultists: sometimes they do your work for you." — Winter [Scryfall: flavor_text field]. The speaker is Winter, Misanthropic Guide (DSK-240) — a Legendary Creature (Human Warlock) who served as the House's guide for survivors navigating Duskmourn's halls, and who struck a doomed contract with Valgavoth to deliver four lives in exchange for an escape door. [Scryfall: Winter, Misanthropic Guide DSK-240; BBL corpus: Betrayer's Bargain trivia via valgavoth.md]
sources: WebSearch: Winter Misanthropic Guide character info

trivia_bullets.text: Valgavoth's Faithful is a 1/1 Human Cleric for {B} whose activated ability ({3}{B}, sacrifice) returns any creature card from your graveyard to the battlefield — a sorcery-speed reanimation engine on a disposable body. EDHREC rank 7,942 as of Scryfall data, meaning it sees limited competitive Commander inclusion but is a thematic fit in sacrifice-and-reanimate builds. Legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, and Commander. [Scryfall: edhrec_rank 7942, legalities]

Artist: Jodie Muir. The card was previewed September 3, 2024 via the official Wizards of the Coast YouTube channel (source_uri per Scryfall preview data). The moth and butterfly motifs in the art are canonically tied to Valgavoth's visual identity — the character node in the BBL corpus cites 'Infernal Phantom' and 'Say Its Name' as the two corpus cards that specify the moth-demon iconography explicitly. [Scryfall: preview field; BBL corpus: valgavoth.md]
